ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Ti la le ta dɛna wuu gogo kaareŋa bunuu zaanuurɛ bɔba, ti a le yehe ta nyɛ ti basɛba zɛ'a, ti a yele ba yeti, ‘Ŋwani ti ya zɛ'a ka'lam yoo beere n yilegɛ daarɛ?’
translated: And they said to you, grandmother, that the children should truly know the one; and it was said that the people say, and they asked them, "Who are you, this one that we should seek?"

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: Ti a zahereba pia la ayima la kiŋɛ Galilii, zoore se'ere ti Yezu pa'alɛ ba la.
translated: We have earnestly prayed to our Lord God in Galilee, that all the sorrows Jesus has taken away from us.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Se'ere en sɔi la, ba ni ba' n mɛ la bãhi la zɔtɔ, gee a ni kɔ' zɔtɔ la mɛ gee kɔ' bãhi la mɛ zɔra zɔra, ti nɛra ka bɔna tara paŋa wan gi' en.
translated: Se'ere, in your house, we come and I go to the market path with my mother; say I take my path and take my market quickly, so that people bring good here one by one to them.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: La ani wuu Nanginɛ biileho wa en pɔ'ɛ biila woo puan se'em wa ti ba dikɛ burɛ;
translated: This year, Nangin has arrived for us, and we plant yams, but the rain has failed; we have not had enough.

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: ti ban boi bini dita la, ti a yele ba, yeti, “N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, yamam ayima wan koohe mam.”
translated: You come here, friends, and say to me, you call now, so, "I tell you to listen oh, my heart is very sad, friends."

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɛrɔd n ta baŋɛ ti baŋereba la pã'ahɛ en mɛ la, a ukɛ mɛ kankaŋi, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba doohe kɔrum bibudibeto sɛba woo n boi Bɛtelehɛm la ka kɔrɔmkɔrɔm za'a, kɔmsɛba n de yuuma ayi' bii sɛba n pun ka paaɛ bala, doohe la hansɛka ti a wum ti baŋereba duma la tɔgɛ bo' en la.
translated: Herod had the innocents slaughtered among the people and mine, and they commanded me secretly to bring the young and tender children so that they might destroy those in Bethlehem with great terror; but I went searching the houses to find and take them, for in those days it was said that they had heard of the innocent children to slay them.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Nɛra kan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra kpɛ'ɛnduma bayi', se'ere en sɔi la, a wan ba'afum yaaɛ ayima gee nɔŋɛ ayima, bii a ba' tuna bɔ'ɔra ayima gee bahera ayima. Ya kan ta'am dɛna yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la lɔgerɔ buyina. Da fabele ya
translated: Now people talk about our village's current problems, but they don't pay attention; we don't support those who help the youth, just as we don't support our village's youth who help the elderly. They say we must work together for the land properly. May there be peace.

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Bala, mam han tara Beelezebule sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi? Bala zuo, ya mɛhi zahereba la en wan gakɛ ya.
translated: Brother, I have seen Beelzebub, the constant sinner. Tell me, why in this world are the constant sinners like that? Brother, come, I will tell you the truth about a person you know.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Ti nɔtɔgeheba pumparenduma zo'e zo'e wan wa'am na pa'ahɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba.
translated: Don't forget the hand pump always; we need that water.

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Ti la iŋɛ fii wa ti nɛreba la n ze' la paaɛ Pita zi'an yele en yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ fum dela ba ayima. Fum tugum n pa'alɛ.”
translated: He told us that in the market where I go, there is a name called Pita, and the people say, “Listen, you do good work. I will help you.”

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Gee ya han mi' ni yetɔgebuna wa vuurɛ. ‘Mam eere la nimbɔ'zɔrɛ gee dagena kaabegɔ,’ ya kan gakɛ ni sɛba en ka galum la.
translated: Tell me, what you said to me are lies. “Mother, they told me in the house that the children are sick,” she said when she came to visit.

ref: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
text: Ba'ahegɔ za'a, a tum la a mɛa dayua yeti, ‘Ba wan na'ahɛ en.’
translated: Hear this, they say we keep supplicating, "They say people don't have it."

ref: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
text: Ti ba yele en yeti, “Bɛtelehɛm n boi Yudiya puan, Se'ere n sɔi la, nɔtɔgeha la n gulehɛ se'em biŋe n bala:
translated: When they called him by name, he said, "I am from Bethlehem in Judea; here I was born, and I have come to worship the one who was born."

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: bii fum pɔ timpaberɛ wa, se'ere sɔi la, Yinɛ nama dafeere n bala, bii fum pɔ Yerusalɛm, se'ere en sɔi la, Yerusalɛm dela na'kãtɛ la tiŋa.
translated: We pray with your mercy, may we enter now; You great Lord in peace, we pray for Jerusalem, may it enter now; Jerusalem is the eternal dwelling place.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Ya wan baŋɛ ba doohe ba wala puan. Ba pi'ihi ta'ama la zaahi tiŋa, bii ba pi'iha mu'uro tuntuuro tiŋa?
translated: You want us to go to the market and be with the people? We see all these houses here, but do we see all those far away ones?

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.

translated: Herod commanded that all the boys born in Bethlehem and its vicinity be killed, and he ordered all the people to be counted, so that his children might live on into future generations—since they had been born in Bethlehem—and there was great fear. But I went to search for the houses, only to find them empty, for he had sent those whom he had ordered to kill the newborn children and them to ensure their survival.

translated: Now we say our important work now, but they do not do; we want to protect our lives and also our people, as we do our work and their lives. They say we should know because this is not much danger. So let us go.

translated: Brother, I heard they speak of Beelzebub, those who constantly worship—yet you, in this world, every day, do you know those who constantly worship? Brother, listen, people say in the village someone opposes them.

translated: Don't forget the things we discussed always every day when you come, we remember, you know.
